title: muehlenbeckia tiliifolia var. glabra
intro: Muehlenbeckia tiliifolia var. glabra, also known as the hairy or glabrous five hearts or simply five hearts, is a type of flowering plant in the family Polygonaceae, native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Muehlenbeckia tiliifolia var. glabra are numerous, including the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an anti-arthritic agent. In herbal preparations, it is often used in the form of a tincture or infusion, and can also be applied topically as a poultice or ointment to aid in the healing process.
